title: alchemilla flabellata subsp. carniolica
intro: Alchemilla flabellata subsp. carniolica, also known as the Alpine Lady's Mantle, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is a perennial herb native to the mountains of central and eastern Europe, characterized by its large, rounded leaves and delicate white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Alchemilla flabellata subsp. carniolica can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or topical cream, and is said to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
